schemas.description: Git operations such as push, clean, reset, diff, checkout, switch, and status.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: Git verb parsed after global git options.

fields.name: remote
fields.description: Remote positional for commands such as git push.

fields.name: branch
fields.description: Branch positional for push, checkout, or switch.

fields.name: ref
fields.description: Ref positional for push, reset, checkout, or switch.

fields.name: force
fields.description: For git push, true only when --force or -f is present. For git clean, true when -f or --force is present.

fields.name: force_with_lease
fields.description: For git push, true when --force-with-lease is present.

fields.name: force_if_includes
fields.description: For git push, true when --force-if-includes is present.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized git option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized git option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the git parser.

examples.title: Deny destructive force pushes
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: git
        semantic:
          verb: push
          force: true

notes: `force`, `force_with_lease`, and `force_if_includes` are separate git push fields; use all three when a policy should cover every force-like push syntax.
`flags_contains` and `flags_prefixes` inspect parser-recognized option tokens, not raw argv words. GenericParser fallback never satisfies semantic flags.

schemas.description: AWS CLI service, operation, profile, region, endpoint, and dry-run matching.

fields.name: service
fields.description: AWS service name such as s3 or iam.

fields.name: profile
fields.description: AWS profile selected by --profile or AWS_PROFILE.

fields.name: region
fields.description: AWS region selected by --region or environment.

fields.name: dry_run
fields.description: True when --dry-run is present, false when --no-dry-run is present, and unset when neither form is recognized.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized AWS option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized AWS option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the AWS parser.

examples.title: Ask for IAM writes
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: aws
        semantic:
          service: iam

schemas.description: Kubernetes verb, resource, namespace, context, filename, selector, and container matching.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: kubectl verb such as get, apply, delete, or exec.

fields.name: subverb
fields.description: Secondary action for compound kubectl commands.

fields.name: namespace_missing
fields.description: True when no namespace was selected.

fields.name: selector_missing
fields.description: True when no selector was selected.

fields.name: dry_run
fields.description: True when --dry-run or a --dry-run value other than none is present; false when --dry-run=none is present; unset when absent.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized kubectl option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized kubectl option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the kubectl parser.

examples.title: Deny production deletes
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: kubectl
        semantic:
          verb: delete
          namespace: prod

schemas.description: GitHub CLI api, pr, issue, repo, release, secret, search, workflow, auth, and run operations.

fields.name: area
fields.description: Top-level gh area such as api, pr, issue, repo, release, secret, search, workflow, auth, or run.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: gh subcommand verb inside the selected area.

fields.name: repo
fields.description: Repository selected by -R or --repo, or the target repository positional for gh repo commands.

fields.name: endpoint_contains
fields.description: Endpoint substrings that must be present.

fields.name: paginate
fields.description: True when gh api --paginate is present.

fields.name: input
fields.description: True when gh api --input is present.

fields.name: silent
fields.description: True when gh api --silent is present.

fields.name: include_headers
fields.description: True when gh api -i or --include is present.

fields.name: field_keys_contains
fields.description: gh api -F/--field keys that must be present.

fields.name: raw_field_keys_contains
fields.description: gh api -f/--raw-field keys that must be present.

fields.name: header_keys_contains
fields.description: gh api -H/--header keys that must be present.

fields.name: pr_number
fields.description: Pull request number positional for gh pr commands.

fields.name: issue_number
fields.description: Issue number positional for gh issue commands.

fields.name: secret_name
fields.description: Secret name positional for gh secret commands.

fields.name: tag
fields.description: Release tag positional for gh release commands.

fields.name: workflow_name
fields.description: Workflow name positional for gh workflow commands.

fields.name: workflow_id
fields.description: Workflow ID positional for gh workflow commands.

fields.name: search_type
fields.description: Search type for gh search commands, such as code, commits, issues, prs, or repos.

fields.name: query_contains
fields.description: Substring that must be present in the gh search query.

fields.name: label_in
fields.description: Issue labels selected by -l or --label; at least one listed label must be present.

fields.name: assignee_in
fields.description: Issue assignees selected by -a or --assignee; at least one listed assignee must be present.

fields.name: title_contains
fields.description: Substring that must be present in the issue title selected by -t or --title.

fields.name: body_contains
fields.description: Substring that must be present in the issue body selected by -b or --body.

fields.name: draft
fields.description: True when gh pr create --draft is present.

fields.name: prerelease
fields.description: True when gh release create/edit --prerelease is present.

fields.name: latest
fields.description: True when gh release create/view --latest is present.

fields.name: fill
fields.description: True when gh pr create --fill is present.

fields.name: force
fields.description: True for gh pr checkout --force or -f, and gh run rerun --force.

fields.name: admin
fields.description: True when gh pr merge --admin is present.

fields.name: auto
fields.description: True when gh pr merge --auto is present.

fields.name: delete_branch
fields.description: True when gh pr merge --delete-branch is present.

fields.name: merge_strategy
fields.description: Merge strategy selected by gh pr merge.

fields.name: run_id
fields.description: Run ID positional for gh run commands.

fields.name: failed
fields.description: True when gh run rerun --failed is present.

fields.name: job
fields.description: Job selected by gh run rerun --job.

fields.name: debug
fields.description: True when gh run rerun --debug is present.

fields.name: exit_status
fields.description: True when gh run view --exit-status is present.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized gh option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized gh option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the gh parser.

examples.title: Deny mutating GitHub API calls
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: gh
        semantic:
          area: api
          method_in: [POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE]

schemas.description: Argo CD app operations such as app get, list, diff, sync, rollback, and delete.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: Argo CD action path such as app sync or app rollback.

fields.name: app_name
fields.description: Application name positional for argocd app commands.

fields.name: revision
fields.description: Revision selected by --revision, or rollback revision positional.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized argocd option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized argocd option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the argocd parser.

examples.title: Ask before syncing an app
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: argocd
        semantic:
          verb: app sync

schemas.description: Google Workspace CLI dynamic Discovery and helper commands.

fields.name: service
fields.description: First non-global gws action token, such as drive, gmail, calendar, sheets, docs, chat, auth, schema, events, workflow, modelarmor, or script.

fields.name: resource_path
fields.description: Exact resource path tokens between service and method, such as [files] or [spreadsheets, values].

fields.name: resource_path_contains
fields.description: Resource path tokens that must be present.

fields.name: method
fields.description: Final Discovery method or helper command name, such as list, get, create, delete, update, patch, export, login, +send, or +upload.

fields.name: method_in
fields.description: Allowed gws methods or helper names.

fields.name: helper
fields.description: True when the parsed method starts with +.

fields.name: mutating
fields.description: True for methods inferred to write or change server state.

fields.name: destructive
fields.description: True for methods inferred to delete, clear, trash, or remove data.

fields.name: read_only
fields.description: True for methods inferred to read without mutation.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: Scopes selected by --scopes or -s, split on commas and spaces.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized gws option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized gws option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the gws parser.

examples.title: Allow Drive file listing
examples.yaml:
permission:
  allow:
    - command:
        name: gws
        semantic:
          service: drive
          resource_path: [files]
          method: list

notes: `gws` dynamically builds much of its command surface from Google Discovery Service, so this schema exposes generic service/resource_path/method fields instead of a closed list of API methods.
`mutating`, `destructive`, and `read_only` are conservative method-name inferences; use explicit service, resource_path, and method fields for tighter policies.

schemas.description: Helmfile apply, sync, destroy, diff, environment, file, selector, namespace, and values matching.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: helmfile verb such as apply, sync, destroy, or diff.

fields.name: environment_missing
fields.description: True when no environment was selected.

fields.name: file_missing
fields.description: True when no state file was selected.

fields.name: namespace_missing
fields.description: True when no namespace was selected.

fields.name: kube_context_missing
fields.description: True when no kube context was selected.

fields.name: selector_missing
fields.description: True when no selector was selected.

fields.name: state_values_set_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --state-values-set that must be present.

fields.name: state_values_set_string_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --state-values-set-string that must be present.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized helmfile option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized helmfile option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the helmfile parser.

examples.title: Ask before production destroy
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: helmfile
        semantic:
          verb: destroy
          environment: prod

schemas.description: Helm verb, subverb, release, chart, namespace, kube context, values, set keys, and safety flag matching.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: Top-level Helm command such as install, upgrade, uninstall, status, get, repo, registry, plugin, dependency, package, pull, or push.

fields.name: subverb
fields.description: Second action token for grouped commands such as repo add, registry login, plugin install, dependency update, get values, or show chart.

fields.name: release
fields.description: Release name for release-oriented commands when it can be identified.

fields.name: chart
fields.description: Chart argument for install, upgrade, template, package, pull, push, verify, and lint when it can be identified.

fields.name: namespace_missing
fields.description: True when no namespace was selected.

fields.name: kube_context_missing
fields.description: True when no kube context was selected.

fields.name: install
fields.description: True when helm upgrade uses --install or -i.

fields.name: values_files
fields.description: Values files that must be present.

fields.name: set_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --set that must be present.

fields.name: set_string_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --set-string that must be present.

fields.name: set_file_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --set-file that must be present.

fields.name: repo_name
fields.description: Repository name for helm repo add/remove.

fields.name: repo_url
fields.description: Repository URL for helm repo add.

fields.name: registry
fields.description: Registry host or URL for helm registry login/logout.

fields.name: plugin_name
fields.description: Plugin argument for helm plugin install/update/uninstall/list when straightforward.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized Helm option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized Helm option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the Helm parser.

examples.title: Allow Helm inspection
examples.yaml:
permission:
  allow:
    - command:
        name: helm
        semantic:
          verb_in: [list, status, history, get, show, search, template, lint]

examples.title: Ask before install or upgrade
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: helm
        semantic:
          verb_in: [install, upgrade]

schemas.description: Terraform subcommands, workspace/state subcommands, and high-risk infrastructure flags.

fields.name: subcommand
fields.description: Terraform subcommand such as init, validate, plan, apply, destroy, state, or workspace.

fields.name: workspace_subcommand
fields.description: Workspace subcommand such as list, show, select, new, or delete.

fields.name: state_subcommand
fields.description: State subcommand such as list, show, mv, rm, pull, push, or replace-provider.

fields.name: targets_contains
fields.description: Targets selected by -target that must be present.

fields.name: replaces_contains
fields.description: Replace addresses selected by -replace that must be present.

fields.name: destroy
fields.description: True for terraform destroy or plan -destroy.

fields.name: plan_file
fields.description: Plan file argument used by terraform apply <planfile>.

fields.name: var_files_contains
fields.description: Variable files selected by -var-file that must be present.

fields.name: upgrade
fields.description: True when terraform init -upgrade is present.

fields.name: reconfigure
fields.description: True when terraform init -reconfigure is present.

fields.name: migrate_state
fields.description: True when terraform init -migrate-state is present.

fields.name: recursive
fields.description: True when terraform fmt -recursive is present.

fields.name: check
fields.description: True when terraform fmt -check is present.

fields.name: force
fields.description: True when a known -force flag is present.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized terraform option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized terraform option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the terraform parser.

examples.title: Allow read-only Terraform commands
examples.yaml:
permission:
  allow:
    - command:
        name: terraform
        semantic:
          subcommand_in: [validate, plan, show, output]

examples.title: Ask before apply
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: terraform
        semantic:
          subcommand: apply

examples.title: Deny auto-approved destroy
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: terraform
        semantic:
          subcommand: destroy
          auto_approve: true

schemas.description: Docker CLI and docker compose commands with high-risk flag detection.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: Top-level docker command, such as run, exec, ps, images, system, or compose.

fields.name: subverb
fields.description: Second action token for command groups such as system prune or compose down.

fields.name: compose_command
fields.description: Command after docker compose, such as up, down, run, exec, logs, ps, or config.

fields.name: image
fields.description: Image name for docker run, pull, push, or build -t when straightforward.

fields.name: container
fields.description: Container name or id for commands such as exec, logs, inspect, rm, and stop when straightforward.

fields.name: service
fields.description: Compose service for docker compose run or exec when straightforward.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Docker context from --context or -c before the verb.

fields.name: host
fields.description: Docker daemon host from -H or --host before the verb.

fields.name: host_prefix
fields.description: Required prefix for Docker daemon host.

fields.name: file
fields.description: Compose file or Dockerfile flag value when straightforward.

fields.name: network_host
fields.description: True when network mode is host.

fields.name: host_mount
fields.description: True when a bind mount source is an absolute host path.

fields.name: root_mount
fields.description: True when a bind mount source is /.

fields.name: docker_socket_mount
fields.description: True when a mount references common Docker socket paths.

fields.name: prune
fields.description: True for docker system/image/volume/builder prune forms.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized docker option tokens that must be present.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized docker option tokens that must start with these prefixes.

examples.title: Allow read-only Docker commands
examples.yaml:
permission:
  allow:
    - command:
        name: docker
        semantic:
          verb_in: [ps, images, inspect, logs, version, info]

examples.title: Deny privileged containers
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: docker
        semantic:
          verb: run
          privileged: true

examples.title: Deny Docker socket mounts
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: docker
        semantic:
          docker_socket_mount: true

notes: Docker semantics are syntactic only; the parser does not inspect images, Dockerfiles, compose files, containers, or daemon state.
`host_mount`, `root_mount`, and `docker_socket_mount` are best-effort checks over command-line mount flags.

argocd.description: Argo CD app operations such as app get, list, diff, sync, rollback, and delete.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: Argo CD action path such as app sync or app rollback.

fields.name: app_name
fields.description: Application name positional for argocd app commands.

fields.name: revision
fields.description: Revision selected by --revision, or rollback revision positional.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized argocd option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized argocd option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the argocd parser.

examples.title: Ask before syncing an app
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: argocd
        semantic:
          verb: app sync

fields.name: region

fields.name: dry_run

fields.name: docker_socket_mount

fields.name: prune

git.description: Git operations such as push, clean, reset, diff, checkout, switch, and status.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: Git verb parsed after global git options.

fields.name: remote
fields.description: Remote positional for commands such as git push.

fields.name: branch
fields.description: Branch positional for push, checkout, or switch.

fields.name: ref
fields.description: Ref positional for push, reset, checkout, or switch.

fields.name: force
fields.description: For git push, true only when --force or -f is present. For git clean, true when -f or --force is present.

fields.name: force_with_lease
fields.description: For git push, true when --force-with-lease is present.

fields.name: force_if_includes
fields.description: For git push, true when --force-if-includes is present.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized git option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized git option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the git parser.

examples.title: Deny destructive force pushes
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: git
        semantic:
          verb: push
          force: true

notes: `force`, `force_with_lease`, and `force_if_includes` are separate git push fields; use all three when a policy should cover every force-like push syntax.
`flags_contains` and `flags_prefixes` inspect parser-recognized option tokens, not raw argv words. GenericParser fallback never satisfies semantic flags.

gws.description: Google Workspace CLI dynamic Discovery and helper commands.

fields.name: service
fields.description: First non-global gws action token, such as drive, gmail, calendar, sheets, docs, chat, auth, schema, events, workflow, modelarmor, or script.

fields.name: resource_path
fields.description: Exact resource path tokens between service and method, such as [files] or [spreadsheets, values].

fields.name: resource_path_contains
fields.description: Resource path tokens that must be present.

fields.name: method
fields.description: Final Discovery method or helper command name, such as list, get, create, delete, update, patch, export, login, +send, or +upload.

fields.name: method_in
fields.description: Allowed gws methods or helper names.

fields.name: helper
fields.description: True when the parsed method starts with +.

fields.name: mutating
fields.description: True for methods inferred to write or change server state.

fields.name: destructive
fields.description: True for methods inferred to delete, clear, trash, or remove data.

fields.name: read_only
fields.description: True for methods inferred to read without mutation.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: Scopes selected by --scopes or -s, split on commas and spaces.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized gws option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized gws option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the gws parser.

examples.title: Allow Drive file listing
examples.yaml:
permission:
  allow:
    - command:
        name: gws
        semantic:
          service: drive
          resource_path: [files]
          method: list

notes: `gws` dynamically builds much of its command surface from Google Discovery Service, so this schema exposes generic service/resource_path/method fields instead of a closed list of API methods.
`mutating`, `destructive`, and `read_only` are conservative method-name inferences; use explicit service, resource_path, and method fields for tighter policies.

helm.description: Helm verb, subverb, release, chart, namespace, kube context, values, set keys, and safety flag matching.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: Top-level Helm command such as install, upgrade, uninstall, status, get, repo, registry, plugin, dependency, package, pull, or push.

fields.name: subverb
fields.description: Second action token for grouped commands such as repo add, registry login, plugin install, dependency update, get values, or show chart.

fields.name: release
fields.description: Release name for release-oriented commands when it can be identified.

fields.name: chart
fields.description: Chart argument for install, upgrade, template, package, pull, push, verify, and lint when it can be identified.

fields.name: namespace_missing
fields.description: True when no namespace was selected.

fields.name: kube_context_missing
fields.description: True when no kube context was selected.

fields.name: install
fields.description: True when helm upgrade uses --install or -i.

fields.name: values_files
fields.description: Values files that must be present.

fields.name: set_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --set that must be present.

fields.name: set_string_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --set-string that must be present.

fields.name: set_file_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --set-file that must be present.

fields.name: repo_name
fields.description: Repository name for helm repo add/remove.

fields.name: repo_url
fields.description: Repository URL for helm repo add.

fields.name: registry
fields.description: Registry host or URL for helm registry login/logout.

fields.name: plugin_name
fields.description: Plugin argument for helm plugin install/update/uninstall/list when straightforward.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized Helm option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized Helm option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the Helm parser.

examples.title: Allow Helm inspection
examples.yaml:
permission:
  allow:
    - command:
        name: helm
        semantic:
          verb_in: [list, status, history, get, show, search, template, lint]

examples.title: Ask before install or upgrade
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: helm
        semantic:
          verb_in: [install, upgrade]

helmfile.description: Helmfile apply, sync, destroy, diff, environment, file, selector, namespace, and values matching.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: helmfile verb such as apply, sync, destroy, or diff.

fields.name: environment_missing
fields.description: True when no environment was selected.

fields.name: file_missing
fields.description: True when no state file was selected.

fields.name: namespace_missing
fields.description: True when no namespace was selected.

fields.name: kube_context_missing
fields.description: True when no kube context was selected.

fields.name: selector_missing
fields.description: True when no selector was selected.

fields.name: state_values_set_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --state-values-set that must be present.

fields.name: state_values_set_string_keys_contains
fields.description: Keys selected by --state-values-set-string that must be present.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized helmfile option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized helmfile option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the helmfile parser.

examples.title: Ask before production destroy
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: helmfile
        semantic:
          verb: destroy
          environment: prod

kubectl.description: Kubernetes verb, resource, namespace, context, filename, selector, and container matching.

fields.name: verb
fields.description: kubectl verb such as get, apply, delete, or exec.

fields.name: subverb
fields.description: Secondary action for compound kubectl commands.

fields.name: namespace_missing
fields.description: True when no namespace was selected.

fields.name: selector_missing
fields.description: True when no selector was selected.

fields.name: dry_run
fields.description: True when --dry-run or a --dry-run value other than none is present; false when --dry-run=none is present; unset when absent.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized kubectl option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized kubectl option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the kubectl parser.

examples.title: Deny production deletes
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: kubectl
        semantic:
          verb: delete
          namespace: prod

terraform.description: Terraform subcommands, workspace/state subcommands, and high-risk infrastructure flags.

fields.name: subcommand
fields.description: Terraform subcommand such as init, validate, plan, apply, destroy, state, or workspace.

fields.name: workspace_subcommand
fields.description: Workspace subcommand such as list, show, select, new, or delete.

fields.name: state_subcommand
fields.description: State subcommand such as list, show, mv, rm, pull, push, or replace-provider.

fields.name: targets_contains
fields.description: Targets selected by -target that must be present.

fields.name: replaces_contains
fields.description: Replace addresses selected by -replace that must be present.

fields.name: destroy
fields.description: True for terraform destroy or plan -destroy.

fields.name: plan_file
fields.description: Plan file argument used by terraform apply <planfile>.

fields.name: var_files_contains
fields.description: Variable files selected by -var-file that must be present.

fields.name: upgrade
fields.description: True when terraform init -upgrade is present.

fields.name: reconfigure
fields.description: True when terraform init -reconfigure is present.

fields.name: migrate_state
fields.description: True when terraform init -migrate-state is present.

fields.name: recursive
fields.description: True when terraform fmt -recursive is present.

fields.name: check
fields.description: True when terraform fmt -check is present.

fields.name: force
fields.description: True when a known -force flag is present.

fields.name: flags_contains
fields.description: Parser-recognized terraform option tokens that must be present; this does not scan raw argv words.

fields.name: flags_prefixes
fields.description: Parser-recognized terraform option tokens that must start with these prefixes; this depends on the terraform parser.

examples.title: Allow read-only Terraform commands
examples.yaml:
permission:
  allow:
    - command:
        name: terraform
        semantic:
          subcommand_in: [validate, plan, show, output]

examples.title: Ask before apply
examples.yaml:
permission:
  ask:
    - command:
        name: terraform
        semantic:
          subcommand: apply

examples.title: Deny auto-approved destroy
examples.yaml:
permission:
  deny:
    - command:
        name: terraform
        semantic:
          subcommand: destroy
          auto_approve: true
